goal: Job queue: DB schema + Go models for persistent task queue

acceptance_criteria.description: Migration SQL creates migration.job_queue table with columns: job_id, engagement_id, tenant_id, job_type, payload JSONB, status (PENDING/RUNNING/COMPLETED/FAILED/RETRYING), priority, max_retries, retry_count, last_error, created_at, started_at, completed_at, next_retry_at
Go model types defined: Job, JobStatus enum, JobType enum (BATCH_EXECUTE, RECONCILE, PROFILE, PARALLEL_RUN, CDC_SYNC), JobPayload interface
DB query functions: EnqueueJob, DequeueNext (SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED), UpdateJobStatus, GetJobsByEngagement, GetJobByID
RLS policy on job_queue table scoped to tenant_id via engagement_members
DequeueNext uses advisory lock or SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED to prevent double-pickup in concurrent workers

critical: RLS enforced on job_queue table — same pattern as all migration tables
tenant_id column present and NOT NULL
DequeueNext prevents double-pickup under concurrency (SKIP LOCKED or advisory lock)
Job status transitions are enforced (PENDING→RUNNING, RUNNING→COMPLETED|FAILED|RETRYING)
Migration file numbered correctly after existing migrations (check latest number)
high: Index on (status, priority, created_at) for efficient dequeue
Index on (engagement_id) for per-engagement job listing
payload is JSONB, not TEXT — allows DB-level querying of job params
next_retry_at populated using exponential backoff formula on RETRYING transition
Tests use sqlmock or short-mode compatible patterns
medium: Dead letter queue concept (jobs exceeding max_retries move to permanent failure)
